report_name: Internal Breach of 'The Gentlemen' Ransomware Group Exposes Infrastructure, Chats, and Victim Data

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: A ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group active since 2025 that operates an affiliate-based model. The group suffered a major internal breach of its systems, exposing its operations, infrastructure, and a victim list of over 1,570 organizations. Despite the OPSEC failure, the group continues its operations.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware deployment by affiliates.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The Gentlemen group and its affiliates primarily gain initial access by targeting and exploiting unpatched vulnerabilities in internet-facing systems.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: Affiliates of The Gentlemen ransomware group used tools specifically designed to disable or bypass endpoint detection and response (EDR) and antivirus solutions to evade defenses.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Gentlemen's ransomware is capable of encrypting a wide range of environments, including Windows, Linux, NAS devices, and VMware ESXi servers, to cause widespread disruption.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The group focused on credential abuse techniques for lateral movement and privilege escalation after gaining initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The group's targeting of VMware ESXi environments includes activities like snapshot deletion to inhibit system recovery.

malware.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload provided by 'The Gentlemen' RaaS platform to its affiliates. It is capable of encrypting Windows, Linux, NAS, and VMware ESXi environments.

identities.name: BreachForums
identities.description: A hacking forum that The Gentlemen ransomware group partnered with after their internal breach.

identities.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware Victims
identities.description: A group of over 1,570 organizations compromised by The Gentlemen ransomware group, as revealed by the group's internal database breach.

infrastructure.name: The Gentlemen Backend Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The backend systems of The Gentlemen RaaS platform, which were compromised and exposed internal data.

infrastructure.name: The Gentlemen Leak Site
infrastructure.description: The public data leak site used by The Gentlemen group to pressure victims. The internal database revealed this site only showed a fraction of the total victims.

infrastructure.name: The Gentlemen Negotiation Platform
infrastructure.description: The platform provided by the RaaS group for affiliates to manage negotiations with victims.

indicators.name: EDR or AV Tampering Detected
indicators.pattern:
title: EDR or AV Service Tampering Attempt
id: 2b8a1c9e-7f4d-4b1a-9c8d-3e2f1a0b9c8d
status: experimental
description: Detects attempts to stop or disable common Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) or Antivirus (AV) services, a common tactic used by ransomware groups like 'The Gentlemen'.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/ransomware-group-the-gentlemen-suffers-internal-breach-exposing-operations
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-19
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_sc:
    Image|endswith: '\sc.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' stop '
      - ' delete '
      - ' config '
  selection_net:
    Image|endswith: '\net.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' stop '
  selection_services:
    - 'MsSense'
    - 'Sense'
    - 'WinDefend'
    - 'MsMpEng'
    - 'SavService'
    - 'Sophos Anti-Virus'
    - 'CrowdStrike'
    - 'CarbonBlack'
  condition: (selection_sc or selection_net) and selection_services
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative action, though stopping these services is highly suspicious.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects attempts to stop or disable common EDR and AV services, a technique used by The Gentlemen's affiliates.

indicators.name: ESXi VM Process Kill Command
indicators.pattern:
title: VMware ESXi VM Process Kill
id: 5d1e0f9a-8b7c-4a3d-9e8f-7a6b5c4d3e2f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'esxcli vm process kill' to terminate virtual machine processes on a VMware ESXi host. This is a common precursor to ransomware encryption in virtualized environments.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/ransomware-group-the-gentlemen-suffers-internal-breach-exposing-operations
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-19
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/esxcli'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' vm process kill'
      - ' vm-process kill'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative action for troubleshooting a hung VM.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the use of the 'esxcli vm process kill' command, which is used to inhibit system recovery before encryption.
